idea.hook: Our Solar System is VASTLY bigger than you think!
facts: The inner Solar System, home to all 8 planets, spans only ~30 Astronomical Units (AU) from the Sun. That's 4.5 BILLION km.
The Kuiper Belt, a ring of icy objects beyond Neptune, extends out to ~50 AU (7.5 BILLION km).
The Oort Cloud, our true outer boundary, BEGINS at 2,000 AU (0.03 Light-Years) and reaches a staggering 100,000 AU (1.5 Light-Years!) from the Sun.
idea.payoff: The familiar planets are just a tiny, central speck within a truly immense, distant, icy sphere that makes up the *vast majority* of our cosmic home!
idea.title: Solar System's REAL Size! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: Your Solar System is VASTLY bigger than you think!
scenes.visual: A vibrant, animated visualization of the inner Solar System (Sun, Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune) with their orbits clearly defined. The view is relatively close-up, showing the planets moving.

scenes.text: All 8 Planets are in the INNER SYSTEM: 30 AU (4.5 Billion km)
scenes.visual: The camera quickly zooms out, showing the 8 planets and their orbits becoming a smaller, central point. A clear, labeled ring indicating '30 AU' appears around Neptune's orbit, with '4.5 BILLION KM' next to it. The background darkens, showing more distant stars.

scenes.text: Kuiper Belt: 50 AU (7.5 Billion km) beyond Neptune.
scenes.visual: The view continues to expand outwards. The inner Solar System is now a very small dot in the center. A much wider, denser ring of small icy objects (the Kuiper Belt) becomes visible, extending to a labeled '50 AU' ring. '7.5 BILLION KM' appears next to it. The background is mostly dark space with sparse stars.

scenes.text: Oort Cloud BEGINS at 2,000 AU & reaches 100,000 AU (1.5 Light-Years!)
scenes.visual: A massive, spherical cloud of tiny icy dots (the Oort Cloud) rapidly forms around the now-microscopic inner system and Kuiper Belt. The scale is immense. A labeled inner boundary '2,000 AU' and an outer boundary '100,000 AU (1.5 Light-Years!)' are shown. The entire screen is filled with the vastness of space and this huge cloud.

scenes.text: Our planets are a tiny speck in an IMMENSE, icy cosmic home!
scenes.visual: A final, wide shot emphasizing the overwhelming scale. The entire Solar System (including the Oort Cloud) is shown as a vast, hazy sphere against a backdrop of distant galaxies or a dense star field. The central dot representing the planets is barely discernible, highlighting the true extent.
